---
title: "LRP62: How much of the word-reading gain is *phonics-route-compatible*? (reading-route decomposition)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

{{< include _partials/_header.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_setup.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_convergence.qmd >}}

## Overview

LRP62 is the robust replacement for LRP59's single-mediator estimate. LRP59 asked
how much of the intervention's word-reading effect flows specifically through
**letter-sound** knowledge; that estimate was directionally informative but fragile
(one measure as "the pathway", a very wide interval). Following the project review
(`notes/2026-05-12-project-review.md`, sec. 124/167), LRP62 instead asks how much of
the effect flows through the **phonics route as a whole** — decoding-compatible
skills used *together* — versus a residual **lexical / whole-word-compatible** path.

We decompose the intervention's total effect on word reading $W$ into the **natural
indirect effect (NIE)** transmitted through the phonics route, and the **natural
direct effect (NDE)** — everything else — and report the **phonics-route share**
$= \text{NIE} / \text{Total}$.

::: {.callout-warning title="Mediator and outcome are measured at the same wave (read before the headline)"}
The randomised contrast lives in the t1 → t2 window, so the route composite and
the outcome ($W_{t2}$) are measured **contemporaneously** — temporal precedence of
the route over word reading is **assumed, not observed**, and reverse/simultaneous
influence within the wave cannot be ruled out. The phonics-route share is reported
with this caveat stated alongside it (see the assumptions section). As a
triangulation point, a **non-randomised sensitivity fit with the outcome at t3**
(mediator still at t2) is reported in the temporal-ordering sensitivity section
below.
:::

## The phonics-route composite {#sec-composite}

The mediator is an **equal-weight, standardised-logit composite** of the phonics
measures that have usable spread in the randomised window:

- **Letter-sound knowledge** $L$ (YARC-LSK) and **phoneme blending** $B$.
- **Phonetic spelling** $P$ is **excluded.** The measurement-sensitivity audit
  (`scripts/measurement_audit.py`,
  ) found $P$ floored — 78% of
  children scored zero at t1 and 64% at t2, with only ~36% moving — so it carries
  little usable signal and would only add noise to the composite. Nonword reading is
  likewise floored and is post-only, so it cannot serve as a baseline-conditioned
  mediator.

Each measure's Haldane-logit is standardised on its post distribution (the same
scaler maps the baseline value), the two are averaged per child, and the composite
is standardised to mean 0 / SD 1. Because the composite is continuous, the mediator
leg is a **Normal** model; the outcome leg and the counterfactual NDE/NIE
decomposition are identical to LRP59.

## Phase 0 only — the key design decision

The intervention is a clean randomised contrast **only in phase 0 (t1 → t2)**: group
1 receives the intervention while group 2 is the wait-list control. After t2 both
arms are treated, so `group` is no longer a treatment indicator. LRP62 therefore
uses **the phase-0 transition only** (`phase_mode="itt"`), $n \approx 53$.

Repo sign convention: $G = 2 - \text{group}$, so $G=1$ is the intervention arm and
$G=0$ the wait-list control. All effects below are reported in the
**intervention-helps** direction (intervention − control), so **positive = the
intervention raises reading**.

## Causal structure (DAG)

- **Treatment** $G$ (randomised in phase 0).
- **Mediator** $M$ = the phonics-route composite at t2 (letter-sound + blending),
  with the baseline composite at t1.
- **Outcome** $Y = W_{t2}$ (word reading at t2), with baseline $W_{t1}$.
- Paths: $G \to M \to W_{t2}$ (indirect, phonics route) and $G \to W_{t2}$ (direct,
  residual / lexical-compatible).
- Mediator–outcome confounders $C$ (revised DAG, #246): age $A$, hearing $HS$,
  phonological memory $RW$, speech production $SP$ — pre-treatment causes of the
  route composite and $W_{t2}$.

A joint model fits both legs simultaneously, including a $G \times M$ interaction so
the decomposition admits exposure–mediator interaction. **NDE/NIE are computed by
counterfactual simulation from the posterior (the g-formula), not as a product of
coefficients.**

## Assumptions — read this first {#sec-assumptions}

The decomposition is causal **only under the following assumptions**; the
phonics-route share is *not* assumption-free.

- Randomisation in phase 0 delivers **no unmeasured confounding of $G \to Y$ and of
  $G \to M$** for free.
- **No unmeasured mediator–outcome (route $\to W$) confounding** — the binding,
  unverifiable assumption and the main limitation. Under the revised DAG (#246) the
  admissible confounders are $A$ (age), $HS$ (hearing), $RW$ (phonological memory)
  and $SP$ (speech production), plus the baseline composite and $W_{t1}$. The
  earlier $E$/$R$ (vocab) adjusters are **dropped** as treatment-affected
  ($IG \to TE \to EV$, $IG \to TR \to RV$) — conditioning on them would violate the
  cross-world assumption. Residual confounding by anything unmeasured would still
  bias the NDE/NIE split.
- Confounders are taken at **baseline (t1), not post (t2)** — the **cross-world**
  assumption.
- Consistency and positivity are assumed.
- **Contemporaneous measurement.** The route composite and word reading are both
  measured at t2; there is no temporal precedence of mediator over outcome within
  the window. The decomposition conditions on baselines, but this remains a
  structural limitation shared with LRP59.

::: {.callout-important title="Triangulation, not a precise causal split"}
Read the output below as triangulation. With $n \approx 53$, contemporaneous
mediator/outcome measurement, and the no-unmeasured-confounding assumption, expect
**wide intervals**. "Most of the word-reading gain is phonics-route-compatible, with
a residual lexical share that cannot be ruled out" is the honest result — not a
point percentage. The temporal-ordering sensitivity section below refits with the
**outcome at t3** (mediator at t2) as a further triangulation point for the
same-wave caveat. LRP62 supersedes the single-mediator LRP59 as the way to talk
about "how much is phonics".
:::

{{< include _partials/_priors.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_prior_predictive.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_diagnostics.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_results_mediation.qmd >}}

{{< include _partials/_footer.qmd >}}
